item.title: Galaxy Tab S9: Best cases, screen protectors, and accessories in 2024
item.itunes:title: Galaxy Tab S9: Best cases, screen protectors, and accessories in 2024
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
The Samsung Galaxy Tab S9 is one of the best Android tablets, with a beautiful, high refresh rate AMOLED screen. It comes with the excellent S Pen, so it’s versatile for productivity tasks right out of the box. The Snapdragon 8 Gen 2 for Galaxy chipset is one of the most potent Qualcomm chips on the market, so it’s got the power you need. While the Galaxy Tab S10 series is out now, it seems that the smaller device isn't refreshed yet, so the Tab S9 is still your best bet for a smaller Samsung tablet. You'll want to pick up some accessories to keep that screen pristine and the power topped up. A screen protector is my first suggestion, followed by a case to protect it from falls. There are tons of other accessories for the popular device, so I rounded up some of my favorites.

 Related
Samsung Galaxy Tab S9 vs. Galaxy Tab S8: Is the new tablet worth the upgrade?

Did Samsung unpack anything new for its Galaxy Tab S9 slate?

Best cases with pen holders

  • Editor's choice
    Fintie Hybrid Slim for Galaxy Tab S9

    This hybrid case keeps the stylish aluminum back of the Galaxy Tab S9 in full view thanks to crystal-clear plastic that protects it from scrapes and falls. It also has TPU bumpers for additional fall protection for those vulnerable corners, and a foldable screen cover that can be used as a stand.

    $13 at Amazon
  • Clear with stand
    Spigen Ultra Hybrid Pro for Galaxy Tab S9

    Spigen's Ultra Hybrid Pro is one of our favorite case series, with ultra-clear polycarbonate welded to flexible TPU for the best of both types of protective materials. It also has a foldable cover to use as a kickstand and two places to stow the S Pen safely while not in use.

    $20 at Amazon $55 at Spigen
  • Protective pick
    Poetic Turtleskin for Galaxy Tab S9

    Poetic's Turtleskin range offers some of the best bumper cases for tablets. The thick silicone protects from drops and bumps like a champ, while adding grip. The Galaxy Tab S9 version has a handy clip for keeping the S Pen secure while it charges wirelessly on the back of the tablet.

    $25 at Amazon $19 at Poetic Cases

Best keyboard cases

  • Editor's choice
    Samsung Book Cover Keyboard for Galaxy Tab S9
    $217 $350 Save $133

    Samsung's Book Cover Keyboard for the Galaxy Tab S9 protects your tablet with a magnetically attached Bluetooth keyboard with a touchpad, making it a viable laptop replacement. It also has full-sized keys, a cover to protect the S Pen while it charges wirelessly, and an adjustable stand that adjusts up to 150 degrees to find your best angle.

    $217 at Amazon
  • Slim keyboard
    Samsung Book Cover Keyboard Slim for Galaxy Tab S9

    If the full-sized Book Cover Keyboard is too thick for your tastes, Samsung has a slim version for the Galaxy Tab S9. It has full-sized keys, but the touchpad was dropped to fit into a slimmer case, and you still get an S Pen protective cover. You can also type on other compatible Galaxy devices thanks to wireless sharing.

    $140 at Samsung $135 at Amazon
  • Best value
    ProCase Keyboard Case for Galaxy Tab S9

    This keyboard case from ProCase has a slim folio design, allowing you to prop your tablet up at a comfortable viewing angle. The keyboard is also removable from the case, so you can position it where you want to work. It might not be as refined as the first-party keyboards, but it's substantially cheaper which might be more important to you.

    $23 at Amazon

Best rugged and waterproof cases

  • All-around protection
    UAG Metropolis for Galaxy Tab S9
    $85 $90 Save $5

    UAG’s Metropolis range is great for all-around protection of tablets, including the screen, as it has a flip-cover that does dual duty as a kickstand that props the tablet up at several angles. That cover also keeps the S Pen securely fastened in its carrying slot, so you don't need to worry about misplacing it.

    $85 at Amazon
  • Officially rugged
    Samsung Outdoor Cover for Tab S9
    $38 $60 Save $22

    Samsung’s official rugged case is mil-grade tested for bump and drop protection. The built-in kickstand not only works for portrait and landscape viewing but can also be hung from a hook or used as a carrying handle for more versatility. And it'll keep your S Pen secure while charging with a sturdy clip.

    $60 at Samsung $38 at Best Buy
  • Promoted pick
    Supcase Unicorn Beetle Pro case for Galaxy Tab S9

    Supcase Unicorn Beetle Pro cases have been protecting our devices for years, and fortunately they're here for Samsung's latest premium tablet. This case offers full-body protection including shock absorption, a built-in screen protector, and a handy kickstand. Oh, and there's a grip to keep your S Pen securely fastened on.

    $30 at Amazon

Best screen protectors

  • Editor's choice
    Samsung NotePaper Screen for Galaxy Tab S9
    $26 $45 Save $19

    To get the best S Pen experience on the Galaxy Tab S9, the NotePaper Screen Panel covers the entire screen and makes it feel more like writing on paper. It attaches via magnets and flips out of the way when you want to use your fingers for scrolling or other navigation.

    $26 at Amazon $45 at Samsung
  • Premium pick
    Spigen Tempered Glass Screen Protector for Galaxy Tab S9

    Spigen is one of the most recognizable names in device protection, and its tempered glass screen protectors will save your display from damage. They go on quickly, too, with an included alignment tool to make it super simple to apply. With such a premium tablet, you'll want to protect the touchscreen from damage.

    $40 at Amazon $50 at Spigen
  • Best value
    Supershieldz for Galaxy Tab S9
    $9 $14 Save $5

    Supershieldz has a reputation for good quality screen protectors. These tempered glass protectors will feel like your tablet’s screen while protecting it from scratches and cracks. You also get a two-pack for this price, which is crazy good value, so you'll still be able to protect the Tab S9 even after one protector gets damaged.

    $9 at Amazon

Chargers and accessories

  • Editor's choice
    Satechi 165W USB-C 4-Port PD Charger

    Sometimes you need to charge more than just your tablet, and this four-port 165W charger from Satechi is perfect for the job. It's also sleek and stylish, so it matches the classy looks of the Tab S9. It'll keep your tablet and three more devices charged and ready, for whenever you need them.

    $120 at Amazon $120 at Satechi
  • Power bank
    Anker 313 PowerCore Slim
    $20 $22 Save $2

    Anker's 313 PowerCore Slim 10K has 10,000mAh of capacity, enough to fully recharge your Galaxy Tab S9 once with a little to spare. It's also lightweight and slim and can charge your other devices if needed. That's great for saving space and weight in your bag, so you won't even notice it's there.

    $20 at Amazon $24 at Anker
  • Best Earbuds
    Samsung Galaxy Buds 2 Pro
    $130 $230 Save $100

    You'll want a quality pair of Bluetooth earbuds to listen to media on your Galaxy Tab S9, and the Galaxy Buds 2 Pro are one of the best options for your new device. They've got active noise-cancelling, 3D audio, and a long battery life, and pair effortlessly with your Galaxy tablet so you can enjoy your content.

    $130 at Samsung $130 at Amazon $130 at Best Buy
  • For DeX users
    QGeeM USB-C to HDMI Cable

    You can turn your tablet into a desktop computer replacement with DeX mode and the right adapter, and this inexpensive dongle is just what you need to get going. That will make your Galaxy Tab S9 even more versatile, so you can get work done or watch content on a larger screen.

    $7 at Amazon
  • Quality cables
    Anker USB-C Cable 2-Pack
    $12 $14 Save $2

    Anker's braided USB-C cables are one of the few cables that haven't broken on me in all the years I've been using devices needing USB-C. Pick up a couple, and you'll (probably) never need another cable. They're compatible with the full wired charging speed of the Galaxy Tab S9, and have reinforced nylon shielding for durability.

    $12 at Amazon
  • Stylish stand
    Maxonar Adjustable Tablet Stand

    With the quality of the screen on this tablet, you'll probably want a good stand to place it on your desk or nightstand for the best viewing angles. As a bonus, this one is almost as stylish as the Galaxy Tab S9, so you won't want to hide it in a drawer while not using it.

    $16 at Amazon

The best accessories for the Galaxy Tab S9 depend on what you use it for

The Samsung Galaxy Tab S9 is one of the most premium and versatile tablets you can get, but you’ll still need a few accessories to get the most from your new device. The first thing I recommend you do is make sure you have a 45W or above USB-C charger handy. Again, Samsung didn’t include one with this expensive tablet, and our best pick is the Satechi 4-Port PD Charger, which can charge your tablet and other devices at the same time.

The gorgeous surfaces of the tablet deserve some protection, whether that’s a screen protector, a protective case, or an all-around case that protects every angle. Cases are widely available now, including updated versions of our favorite keyboard case, the Samsung Book Cover Keyboard, and others from well-known brands such as Spigen, Poetic, and UAG. Please note that Samsung changed the dimensions slightly on the Tab S9 and redesigned the camera bump, so cases for the Tab S8 won't work with the latest tablet.

There are also so many other accessories you could pick up, from wireless earbuds to speakers, stands, and keyboard cases for when you want to get some work done on the go. Or you could catch up on your Netflix backlog since that AMOLED screen deserves your attention all day long.

On the other hand, if you decide that the ultra-premium Galaxy Tab S9 Ultra is better for your needs, you’ll want to pick up one of the best S9 Ultra cases to protect your lovely purchase.

 Related
How the Galaxy Tab S9 and Fold 5 play nice to form a Samsung mobile ecosystem

Samsung is one of the few Android makers right now to compete with Apple's ecosystem

The Samsung Galaxy Tab S9 is one of the best Android tablets, with a beautiful, high refresh rate AMOLED screen. It comes with the excellent S Pen, so it’s versatile for productivity tasks right out of the box. The Snapdragon 8 Gen 2 for Galaxy chipset is one of the most potent Qualcomm chips on the market, so it’s got the power you need. While the Galaxy Tab S10 series is out now, it seems that the smaller device isn't refreshed yet, so the Tab S9 is still your best bet for a smaller Samsung tablet. You'll want to pick up some accessories to keep that screen pristine and the power topped up. A screen protector is my first suggestion, followed by a case to protect it from falls. There are tons of other accessories for the popular device, so I rounded up some of my favorites.

 Related
Samsung Galaxy Tab S9 vs. Galaxy Tab S8: Is the new tablet worth the upgrade?

Did Samsung unpack anything new for its Galaxy Tab S9 slate?

Best cases with pen holders

  • Editor's choice
    Fintie Hybrid Slim for Galaxy Tab S9

    This hybrid case keeps the stylish aluminum back of the Galaxy Tab S9 in full view thanks to crystal-clear plastic that protects it from scrapes and falls. It also has TPU bumpers for additional fall protection for those vulnerable corners, and a foldable screen cover that can be used as a stand.

    $13 at Amazon
  • Clear with stand
    Spigen Ultra Hybrid Pro for Galaxy Tab S9

    Spigen's Ultra Hybrid Pro is one of our favorite case series, with ultra-clear polycarbonate welded to flexible TPU for the best of both types of protective materials. It also has a foldable cover to use as a kickstand and two places to stow the S Pen safely while not in use.

    $20 at Amazon $55 at Spigen
  • Protective pick
    Poetic Turtleskin for Galaxy Tab S9

    Poetic's Turtleskin range offers some of the best bumper cases for tablets. The thick silicone protects from drops and bumps like a champ, while adding grip. The Galaxy Tab S9 version has a handy clip for keeping the S Pen secure while it charges wirelessly on the back of the tablet.

    $25 at Amazon $19 at Poetic Cases

Best keyboard cases

  • Editor's choice
    Samsung Book Cover Keyboard for Galaxy Tab S9
    $217 $350 Save $133

    Samsung's Book Cover Keyboard for the Galaxy Tab S9 protects your tablet with a magnetically attached Bluetooth keyboard with a touchpad, making it a viable laptop replacement. It also has full-sized keys, a cover to protect the S Pen while it charges wirelessly, and an adjustable stand that adjusts up to 150 degrees to find your best angle.

    $217 at Amazon
  • Slim keyboard
    Samsung Book Cover Keyboard Slim for Galaxy Tab S9

    If the full-sized Book Cover Keyboard is too thick for your tastes, Samsung has a slim version for the Galaxy Tab S9. It has full-sized keys, but the touchpad was dropped to fit into a slimmer case, and you still get an S Pen protective cover. You can also type on other compatible Galaxy devices thanks to wireless sharing.

    $140 at Samsung $135 at Amazon
  • Best value
    ProCase Keyboard Case for Galaxy Tab S9

    This keyboard case from ProCase has a slim folio design, allowing you to prop your tablet up at a comfortable viewing angle. The keyboard is also removable from the case, so you can position it where you want to work. It might not be as refined as the first-party keyboards, but it's substantially cheaper which might be more important to you.

    $23 at Amazon

Best rugged and waterproof cases

  • All-around protection
    UAG Metropolis for Galaxy Tab S9
    $85 $90 Save $5

    UAG’s Metropolis range is great for all-around protection of tablets, including the screen, as it has a flip-cover that does dual duty as a kickstand that props the tablet up at several angles. That cover also keeps the S Pen securely fastened in its carrying slot, so you don't need to worry about misplacing it.

    $85 at Amazon
  • Officially rugged
    Samsung Outdoor Cover for Tab S9
    $38 $60 Save $22

    Samsung’s official rugged case is mil-grade tested for bump and drop protection. The built-in kickstand not only works for portrait and landscape viewing but can also be hung from a hook or used as a carrying handle for more versatility. And it'll keep your S Pen secure while charging with a sturdy clip.

    $60 at Samsung $38 at Best Buy
  • Promoted pick
    Supcase Unicorn Beetle Pro case for Galaxy Tab S9

    Supcase Unicorn Beetle Pro cases have been protecting our devices for years, and fortunately they're here for Samsung's latest premium tablet. This case offers full-body protection including shock absorption, a built-in screen protector, and a handy kickstand. Oh, and there's a grip to keep your S Pen securely fastened on.

    $30 at Amazon

Best screen protectors

  • Editor's choice
    Samsung NotePaper Screen for Galaxy Tab S9
    $26 $45 Save $19

    To get the best S Pen experience on the Galaxy Tab S9, the NotePaper Screen Panel covers the entire screen and makes it feel more like writing on paper. It attaches via magnets and flips out of the way when you want to use your fingers for scrolling or other navigation.

    $26 at Amazon $45 at Samsung
  • Premium pick
    Spigen Tempered Glass Screen Protector for Galaxy Tab S9

    Spigen is one of the most recognizable names in device protection, and its tempered glass screen protectors will save your display from damage. They go on quickly, too, with an included alignment tool to make it super simple to apply. With such a premium tablet, you'll want to protect the touchscreen from damage.

    $40 at Amazon $50 at Spigen
  • Best value
    Supershieldz for Galaxy Tab S9
    $9 $14 Save $5

    Supershieldz has a reputation for good quality screen protectors. These tempered glass protectors will feel like your tablet’s screen while protecting it from scratches and cracks. You also get a two-pack for this price, which is crazy good value, so you'll still be able to protect the Tab S9 even after one protector gets damaged.

    $9 at Amazon

Chargers and accessories

  • Editor's choice
    Satechi 165W USB-C 4-Port PD Charger

    Sometimes you need to charge more than just your tablet, and this four-port 165W charger from Satechi is perfect for the job. It's also sleek and stylish, so it matches the classy looks of the Tab S9. It'll keep your tablet and three more devices charged and ready, for whenever you need them.

    $120 at Amazon $120 at Satechi
  • Power bank
    Anker 313 PowerCore Slim
    $20 $22 Save $2

    Anker's 313 PowerCore Slim 10K has 10,000mAh of capacity, enough to fully recharge your Galaxy Tab S9 once with a little to spare. It's also lightweight and slim and can charge your other devices if needed. That's great for saving space and weight in your bag, so you won't even notice it's there.

    $20 at Amazon $24 at Anker
  • Best Earbuds
    Samsung Galaxy Buds 2 Pro
    $130 $230 Save $100

    You'll want a quality pair of Bluetooth earbuds to listen to media on your Galaxy Tab S9, and the Galaxy Buds 2 Pro are one of the best options for your new device. They've got active noise-cancelling, 3D audio, and a long battery life, and pair effortlessly with your Galaxy tablet so you can enjoy your content.

    $130 at Samsung $130 at Amazon $130 at Best Buy
  • For DeX users
    QGeeM USB-C to HDMI Cable

    You can turn your tablet into a desktop computer replacement with DeX mode and the right adapter, and this inexpensive dongle is just what you need to get going. That will make your Galaxy Tab S9 even more versatile, so you can get work done or watch content on a larger screen.

    $7 at Amazon
  • Quality cables
    Anker USB-C Cable 2-Pack
    $12 $14 Save $2

    Anker's braided USB-C cables are one of the few cables that haven't broken on me in all the years I've been using devices needing USB-C. Pick up a couple, and you'll (probably) never need another cable. They're compatible with the full wired charging speed of the Galaxy Tab S9, and have reinforced nylon shielding for durability.

    $12 at Amazon
  • Stylish stand
    Maxonar Adjustable Tablet Stand

    With the quality of the screen on this tablet, you'll probably want a good stand to place it on your desk or nightstand for the best viewing angles. As a bonus, this one is almost as stylish as the Galaxy Tab S9, so you won't want to hide it in a drawer while not using it.

    $16 at Amazon

The best accessories for the Galaxy Tab S9 depend on what you use it for

The Samsung Galaxy Tab S9 is one of the most premium and versatile tablets you can get, but you’ll still need a few accessories to get the most from your new device. The first thing I recommend you do is make sure you have a 45W or above USB-C charger handy. Again, Samsung didn’t include one with this expensive tablet, and our best pick is the Satechi 4-Port PD Charger, which can charge your tablet and other devices at the same time.

The gorgeous surfaces of the tablet deserve some protection, whether that’s a screen protector, a protective case, or an all-around case that protects every angle. Cases are widely available now, including updated versions of our favorite keyboard case, the Samsung Book Cover Keyboard, and others from well-known brands such as Spigen, Poetic, and UAG. Please note that Samsung changed the dimensions slightly on the Tab S9 and redesigned the camera bump, so cases for the Tab S8 won't work with the latest tablet.

There are also so many other accessories you could pick up, from wireless earbuds to speakers, stands, and keyboard cases for when you want to get some work done on the go. Or you could catch up on your Netflix backlog since that AMOLED screen deserves your attention all day long.

On the other hand, if you decide that the ultra-premium Galaxy Tab S9 Ultra is better for your needs, you’ll want to pick up one of the best S9 Ultra cases to protect your lovely purchase.

 Related
How the Galaxy Tab S9 and Fold 5 play nice to form a Samsung mobile ecosystem

Samsung is one of the few Android makers right now to compete with Apple's ecosystem

item.title: Here's why you should consider buying an Arm-powered NAS
item.itunes:title: Here's why you should consider buying an Arm-powered NAS
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
Network-attached storage (NAS) comes in all shapes and sizes, some are powered by Intel and AMD x86 processors and others rely on Arm chips. The CPU is a vital component inside the NAS, much like it is with any other electronic device. The more powerful the processor, the faster the server can handle more intensive workloads. You'll also be able to do much more on the same device, limiting how much hardware must be deployed. But if you're looking to save money and power, an Arm-powered NAS can make sense. Here are some reasons to buy one over a NAS with an x86 CPU.

 Related
Best NAS devices in 2024

Expand your PC storage with one of these NAS enclosures

1 It's your first NAS

Getting started with Arm

If this will be the first enclosure you've purchased, an Arm-powered NAS is a good place to start. Using a more affordable enclosure ensures you won't purchase an overkill model for your needs. At the very least, a NAS will store data. It's all in the name, network-attached storage. An Arm processor is ample for storing data and sharing it between all connected hardware on the local network. Only when you plan to use more intensive applications will the lack of CPU power (and support) matter. An Arm NAS can help you learn everything there is to know about NAS, networking, and more.

2 Arm-powered NAS are more affordable

Save the most with an Arm NAS

Arm processors are generally more affordable than their AMD or Intel counterparts, especially in the NAS world. You'll often find Arm processors inside budget-friendly enclosures from manufacturers such as Asustor, Synology, and TerraMaster. The cost of a NAS can be expensive without factoring in storage drives. Most NAS models are diskless, meaning you'll need to source drives separately, which can increase the initial cost when factoring in the premium applied to NAS-rated HDDs and SSDs. Going for an Arm NAS can help mitigate this by reducing server costs.

3 The better choice for data hoarding

You may not need a powerful CPU

If you don't need the NAS to do much more than store files, an Arm processor will be sufficient for handling multiple simultaneous connections. Moving data between devices and managing active connections does not tax the system too much and you shouldn't encounter sluggish performance. It's worth noting that because Arm processors are found on more affordable NAS, there's a good chance only 1Gb networking will be present in the form of one or two ports. That's not to say an Arm CPU is weak and can't handle anything you throw at it. An Arm-powered NAS can run many of the same apps as an x86 CPU.

There are some excellent NAS enclosures where the manufacturer has pushed the Arm CPU further with better specifications. The Asus Drivestore 2 Pro Gen2 (AS3302T v2) is a perfect example. It has 2.5Gb networking, 2GB of DDR4 RAM, and expansion support for up to 14 drive bays.

 Related
Asustor Drivestor 2 Pro Gen2 (AS3302T v2) review: This ARM-powered NAS is positively deceiving

Beefy 2.5Gb networking, support for up to 14 bays, this budget-friendly NAS is a monster.

4 Arm CPUs generally use less power than x86 chips

Save on your utility bills

The processor inside your smartphone is based on Arm. Apple's M processors for all its hardware are also based on Arm. This is because the architecture is incredibly efficient. My trusty M2 MacBook Air can go for 12 hours of general computing without hitting me with a low battery warning notification. Arm chips inside a NAS can use notably less power than an AMD or Intel CPU. If you're considering a DIY build, this is something to factor in when looking at off-the-shelf PC parts. An AMD Ryzen or Intel Core CPU can draw more power than an entire turnkey pre-built NAS alone.

5 Create a power-efficient home surveillance or media server

An Arm NAS doesn't have to be limited

An Arm processor may not be as powerful as those found inside an x86 NAS but it can still run some of the more popular packages, including surveillance. Want to configure a custom security solution for the home or office? An Arm-powered NAS can do just that! This keeps costs down, which will be inflated due to IP cameras and any necessary licensing. Once up and running, you'll have a compact system that sucks little power and shouldn't produce much heat in the process. While you can write off any transcoding through media apps like Plex and Jellyfin, it's still possible to use Arm for other uses.

Arm is a solid choice for NAS

AMD or Intel processors will be more powerful for NAS deployment, but you must factor in additional cost, energy draw, and heat generation. When building a DIY enclosure, it can prove challenging to configure an Arm NAS with off-the-shelf parts, though boards are available and some operating systems can be installed on an Arm-powered system.

AMD or Intel processors will be more powerful for NAS deployment, but you must factor in additional cost, energy draw, and heat generation. When building a DIY enclosure, it can prove challenging to configure an Arm NAS with off-the-shelf parts, though boards are available and some operating systems can be installed on an Arm-powered system.
